---
title: "Teaching"
---

## Teaching experience

### [University Name] — Teaching Assistant

| Year | Course | Role | Materials |
|------|--------|------|-----------|
| 2025–26 | Fluid Mechanics II | TA, Lab instructor | [Problem sets](teaching/fluids2/) |
| 2024–25 | Numerical Methods for Engineers | TA | [Julia tutorials](teaching/numerical-methods/) |
| 2023–24 | Introduction to CFD | Lab instructor | [OpenFOAM guides](teaching/intro-cfd/) |


## Student supervision

- **Student Name** (MSc, 2025): *Thesis title*
- **Student Name** (BSc, 2024): *Project title*


## Teaching materials

::: {.callout-tip}
## Open-source teaching
I believe in making teaching materials freely available. 
All resources below are shared under a CC-BY license.
:::

### Julia for Scientific Computing — Tutorial series

A set of introductory notebooks for engineering students transitioning to Julia:

1. [Basics: Arrays, loops, and functions](teaching/julia-tutorials/01-basics.qmd)
2. [Linear algebra and sparse systems](teaching/julia-tutorials/02-linear-algebra.qmd)
3. [ODE solvers with DifferentialEquations.jl](teaching/julia-tutorials/03-ode-solvers.qmd)
4. [Plotting with CairoMakie](teaching/julia-tutorials/04-plotting.qmd)

### MATLAB quick references

- [FFT and spectral methods cheat sheet](teaching/matlab/fft-cheatsheet.pdf)
- [Post-processing DNS data](teaching/matlab/dns-postprocessing.pdf)
